hypothesis: If the Force Delta metric captures genuine physical inconsistency in the learned potential energy surface, then incorporating it as a regularization term during fine-tuning of pre-trained direct-force NNIPs on domain-specific data will reduce the divergence between conservative and non-conservative force predictions, and this reduction will correlate with improved transferability to chemically similar out-of-distribution structures as measured by lower DFT force errors, compared to fine-tuning with standard loss functions alone.
